God is patient, but he will not be patient forever with a provoking people. Remembering the mercies we received in the past, like the produce of the earth from earlier years, should make us submissive to God’s will when we experience disappointments later on. The Lord has many ways of humbling a sinful nation. Whatever trouble we are under, we should earnestly seek God for forgiveness of sin. Sin will quickly reduce a great people. What will become of Israel if the hand that should raise him is stretched out against him? See the power of prayer. See what a blessing praying people are to a land. See how ready and swift God is to show mercy; how he waits to be gracious. Israel was a wall, a strong wall, which God himself raised as a defense to his sanctuary. The Lord now seems to stand upon this wall. He measures it; it appears to be bowing and bulging. Thus God would bring the people of Israel to trial, would discover their wickedness; and there will come a time when those who have been spared often will be spared no longer. But the Lord still calls Israel his people. The prophet’s repeated prayer and success should lead us to seek the Savior.

It is nothing new for those accusing others to misrepresent them as enemies of the king and kingdom, as traitors to their prince, and troublers of the land, when they are actually the best friends to both. Those who make wealth their godliness and are governed by hopes of riches and advancement are ready to think that these are powerful motives for others also. But those who have a warrant from God, like Amos, ought not to fear people. If God, who sent him, had not strengthened him, he could not have set his face as a flint. The Lord often chooses the weak and foolish things of the world to confound the wise and mighty. But no fervent prayers or self-denying labors can bring proud sinners to accept faithful reproofs and warnings. And all who oppose or despise God’s word must expect fatal effects on their souls unless they repent.
